user_query:
I need a small docs site generated from Markdown with embedded CSS. Please do the following:

1) Read input/docs_config.json to determine which Markdown files under input/ to render. The JSON has a documents array with objects like: {"source": "input/<file>.md", "title": "<HTML title>", "theme": "light|dark", "output": "output/<file>.html"}.

2) For each document, convert the Markdown to a self-contained HTML file with the specified theme and set the HTML <title> to the provided title. Use only relative paths and write the HTML files exactly to the output paths listed in the config (e.g., output/weekly_notes.html, output/changelog.html, output/api_guide.html). Make sure code blocks and tables render correctly.

3) Before processing, quickly check the current CPU and memory load and save a brief JSON status report to output/system_health.json with at least these fields: status (ok|warning|critical), recommendation (CONTINUE|PAUSE), cpu (with a load_percent field), and memory (with a used_percent field).

4) Create a landing page at output/index.html that:
   - Includes an "Overview" section introducing that these pages were generated from Markdown into HTML with embedded CSS (2–3 sentences).
   - Lists each generated document in the same order as in input/docs_config.json with: an anchor link to the corresponding output HTML file, the configured title, and the theme used (light or dark) clearly visible next to each link.
   - Uses only relative links to the generated files.

5) Do not use any absolute paths or external resources. Everything must work locally from the output/ folder.

Inputs to read are in input/. Write all generated files to output/ as described.
